# Pretty CLI reference

This reference is generated from the built `pretty` command registry in `prettygo/cmd/pretty/help.go`.

## Top-level help

```text
pretty — prettyd CLI

Usage:
  pretty [global flags] <command> [args]
  pretty help <command>

Session ids may be full ids or unique prefixes from `pretty ls`.

Daily workflows:
  new                      create an interactive session
  run                      run a command in a headless lane
  ls                       list sessions
  sessions                 list agent sessions and headless lanes
  lanes                    list headless lanes
  send                     send text and Enter to a session
  ask                      send, wait, and print the reply
  wait                     wait for session idle or lane exit
  last                     print recent conversation or lane output
  search                   search normalized session chat history
  status                   show a compact session status card
  kill                     terminate sessions or lanes
  recover                  inspect or reopen recoverable sessions
  recall                   inspect integration recall data
  snap                     print the current terminal buffer
  tail                     print or follow recent terminal lines
  transcript               print the full conversation transcript
  input                    alias for send
  keys                     send a named key to a session
  resize                   resize a session PTY
  verdict                  read or emit an explicit producer verdict
  move                     move a session to another endpoint
  adopt                    bind an existing conversation into Pretty

Models and interactive:
  model                    switch an idle session model
  models                   list the live Codex model catalog
  attach                   attach a raw two-way terminal stream

Admin/operational:
  install                  install and start the development daemon
  uninstall                stop and remove the development daemon
  deploy                   perform the canonical safe update
  lan                      manage same-network access
  remote                   manage tailnet-only remote access
  token                    print the daemon authentication token
  backup                   configure and run session backups
  doctor                   diagnose daemon and session health
  help                     show top-level or command help
  version (--version, -v)  print the CLI version

Global flags (must precede the command):
  --json           machine-friendly output
  --host HOST      prettyd host (default 127.0.0.1)
  --port PORT      prettyd port (default 8787)

Run `pretty help <command>` for usage, options, and examples.
```

## `pretty new`

```text
Usage:
  pretty new [--tool claude|codex|shell] [--cwd P] [--name L] [--description PURPOSE] [options] [args...]

create an interactive session

Create a session. --tool selects a built-in Claude, Codex, or shell preset; --cmd supplies a command directly. --description (alias --desc) records why the session exists. Session controls include --model, --effort, --fast, --on-idle, --wait-ready, and --force.

Examples:
  pretty new --tool claude --cwd ~/work
  pretty new --tool codex --model gpt-5-codex
  pretty new --cmd /bin/zsh

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty run`

```text
Usage:
  pretty run [--name N] [--description PURPOSE] [--cwd D] [--spec FILE] [--wait [--output]] -- <cmd args...>

run a command in a headless lane

Create a headless lane for the command following the first -- separator. --description (alias --desc) records why the lane exists. Every child argument after that separator is passed unchanged. Without --wait, print the lane id and return. --wait blocks for completion and propagates the child exit code; --output prints the captured output tail.

Examples:
  pretty run -- make test
  pretty run --name lint --wait --output -- npm run lint
  pretty --json run --wait -- sh -c 'exit 3'

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty ls`

```text
Usage:
  pretty ls [--mine | --all] [-a | --include-exited]

list sessions

List agent sessions known to the daemon. --mine follows PRETTY_OWNER_ID, then the PRETTY_SESSION_ID descendant subtree, then the daemon OS user. The OS-user fallback is user-wide, not invocation-scoped. Exited sessions are hidden by default; -a and --include-exited include them.

Examples:
  pretty ls
  pretty ls --mine
  pretty --json ls

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty sessions`

```text
Usage:
  pretty sessions [--mine | --owner ID | --all] [--include-closed]

list agent sessions and headless lanes

List agent sessions and headless lanes together. --mine follows PRETTY_OWNER_ID, then the PRETTY_SESSION_ID descendant subtree, then the daemon OS user. The OS-user fallback is user-wide, not invocation-scoped. Closed records are hidden unless --include-closed is supplied.

Examples:
  pretty sessions --mine
  pretty sessions --mine --include-closed
  pretty sessions --owner team:mine

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty lanes`

```text
Usage:
  pretty lanes [--all | --mine [--owner ID] | --subtree ID] [--direct] [--detach]

list headless lanes

List retained headless lanes. --mine follows PRETTY_OWNER_ID, then the PRETTY_SESSION_ID descendant subtree, then the daemon OS user. The OS-user fallback is user-wide, not invocation-scoped. --subtree selects session ancestry; --direct limits ancestry to immediate children.

Examples:
  pretty lanes
  pretty lanes --mine
  pretty lanes --subtree 0123abcd --direct

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty send`

```text
Usage:
  pretty send <id> [--timeout D] [--no-wait] [--file PATH] <text...>

send text and Enter to a session

Send a message and Enter. Claude and Codex sessions wait for receipt confirmation by default; --no-wait uses fire-and-forget behavior and --file reads the message body from a UTF-8 file.

Examples:
  pretty send 0123abcd 'Run the focused tests.'
  pretty send 0123abcd --file prompt.md

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty ask`

```text
Usage:
  pretty ask <id> [--timeout D] [--idle D] [--wait-timeout D] <text...>

send, wait, and print the reply

Send a confirmed message to a Claude or Codex session, wait for the reply to finish, and print the last assistant message.

Examples:
  pretty ask 0123abcd 'Summarize the failing test.'
  pretty --json ask 0123abcd --wait-timeout 2m 'Report status.'

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty wait`

```text
Usage:
  pretty wait <id> [<id>... --any] [--idle D] [--timeout D] [condition]

wait for session idle or lane exit

Wait for a session to become idle or a lane to exit. Lane waits propagate the lane exit code. Conditions include --until commit, --until-file-contains FILE STRING, and --until-idle-stable D.

Examples:
  pretty wait 0123abcd --timeout 2m
  pretty wait lane-a lane-b --any
  pretty wait 0123abcd --until commit --timeout 10m

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty last`

```text
Usage:
  pretty last <id> [--role user|assistant] [-n N]

print recent conversation or lane output

For sessions, print recent user and assistant messages from the event log. For completed lanes, print the captured output tail.

Examples:
  pretty last 0123abcd
  pretty last 0123abcd --role assistant -n 1
  pretty --json last 0123abcd

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty search`

```text
Usage:
  pretty search <query> [--session ID] [--role user|assistant] [--tool claude|codex|shell] [-n N] [--regex | --ranked] [--json]

search normalized session chat history

Search chat history across every live and persisted session known to the daemon. Matching is a case-insensitive substring by default; --regex uses a Go regular expression. --ranked opts into BM25-ranked, stemmed search with quoted phrases and AND/OR/NOT boolean operators, and cannot be combined with --regex. Filters are evaluated by the daemon, so --host can search a remote Pretty instance.

Examples:
  pretty search 'migration plan'
  pretty search 'failed|timed out' --regex --role assistant
  pretty search '"migration plan" OR rollback' --ranked
  pretty search needle --session 0123abcd --json

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty status`

```text
Usage:
  pretty status <id>

show a compact session status card

Show session state, tool, working directory, git state, activity timestamps, and the latest explicit verdict when present.

Examples:
  pretty status 0123abcd
  pretty --json status 0123abcd

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty kill`

```text
Usage:
  pretty kill <id> [<id>...]

terminate sessions or lanes

Resolve each id or unique prefix and request termination. The command exits nonzero if a requested target cannot be terminated.

Examples:
  pretty kill 0123abcd
  pretty kill 0123abcd 89abcdef

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty recover`

```text
Usage:
  pretty recover [--all | --reopen [--force]]

inspect or reopen recoverable sessions

List actionable recovery recipes. --all also shows blocked and unresumable lost records with reasons. --reopen creates replacement sessions for eligible records; --force overrides the live or moved conversation guard.

Examples:
  pretty recover
  pretty recover --all
  pretty recover --reopen
  pretty --json recover --reopen --force

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty recall`

```text
Usage:
  pretty recall [<full-session-id> [--raw]]

inspect integration recall data

Show the integration-backed recall view, optionally for one full session id. --raw prints the source payload.

Examples:
  pretty recall
  pretty recall 00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001 --raw

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty snap`

```text
Usage:
  pretty snap <id> [--raw]

print the current terminal buffer

Print the current terminal snapshot. The default cleans terminal control sequences; --raw preserves the daemon response.

Examples:
  pretty snap 0123abcd
  pretty snap 0123abcd --raw

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty tail`

```text
Usage:
  pretty tail <id> [-f] [-n N]

print or follow recent terminal lines

Print the last N terminal lines, defaulting to 50. -f keeps following new output.

Examples:
  pretty tail 0123abcd
  pretty tail 0123abcd -n 200 -f

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty transcript`

```text
Usage:
  pretty transcript <id>

print the full conversation transcript

Print all user and assistant turns decoded from the session event log. Use the global --json flag for structured turns.

Examples:
  pretty transcript 0123abcd
  pretty --json transcript 0123abcd

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty input`

```text
Usage:
  pretty input <id> [send options] <text...>

alias for send

Send text and Enter using the same confirmation behavior and options as pretty send.

Examples:
  pretty input 0123abcd 'Continue.'

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty keys`

```text
Usage:
  pretty keys <id> <esc|up|down|left|right|^c|^d|enter|tab>

send a named key to a session

Translate a supported key name to terminal bytes and send it to the session.

Examples:
  pretty keys 0123abcd esc
  pretty keys 0123abcd ^c

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty resize`

```text
Usage:
  pretty resize <id> <cols> <rows>

resize a session PTY

Resize the terminal associated with a session to the requested columns and rows.

Examples:
  pretty resize 0123abcd 160 48

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty verdict`

```text
Usage:
  pretty verdict <id> | verdict emit <id> [JSON]

read or emit an explicit producer verdict

Print the latest verdict for a session or lane. verdict emit appends a schemaVersion 1 verdict, reading JSON from the argument or standard input.

Examples:
  pretty verdict 0123abcd
  pretty --json verdict emit 0123abcd '{"schemaVersion":1,"verdict":"pass","findings":[]}'

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty move`

```text
Usage:
  pretty move <session> --to <target-endpoint> [--token T] [--dry-run] [--allow-dirty]

move a session to another endpoint

Validate and transfer a session to another Pretty endpoint. --dry-run reports the plan; --allow-dirty permits a dirty working tree.

Examples:
  pretty move 0123abcd --to https://pretty.example
  pretty move 0123abcd --to https://pretty.example --dry-run

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty adopt`

```text
Usage:
  pretty adopt <path-or-uuid> [--force]

bind an existing conversation into Pretty

Adopt an existing conversation path or UUID as a Pretty session. --force overrides the live or moved conversation guard.

Examples:
  pretty adopt 00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001
  pretty adopt ~/.claude/projects/example/session.jsonl --force

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty model`

```text
Usage:
  pretty model <session> <model> [--effort LEVEL]

switch an idle session model

Switch the model, and optionally effort, for an idle supported session.

Examples:
  pretty model 0123abcd sonnet
  pretty model 0123abcd opus --effort high

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty models`

```text
Usage:
  pretty models

list the live Codex model catalog

Query the Codex app-server model catalog, including supported efforts and service tiers. Use the global --json flag for the full structured catalog.

Examples:
  pretty models
  pretty --json models

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty attach`

```text
Usage:
  pretty attach <id>

attach a raw two-way terminal stream

Attach the local terminal to a session. Press Ctrl+Q to detach without terminating the session.

Examples:
  pretty attach 0123abcd

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty install`

```text
Usage:
  pretty install

install and start the development daemon

Register the development prettyd macOS LaunchAgent and start it.

Examples:
  pretty install

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty uninstall`

```text
Usage:
  pretty uninstall

stop and remove the development daemon

Stop and remove the development prettyd macOS LaunchAgent.

Examples:
  pretty uninstall

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty deploy`

```text
Usage:
  pretty deploy [--repo DIR] [--no-pull] [--dry-run]

perform the canonical safe update

Update dependencies, build, smoke-test, restart, health-check, and verify runners. --no-pull skips git pull; --dry-run performs only non-mutating validation.

Examples:
  pretty deploy
  pretty deploy --repo ~/src/pretty-PTY --no-pull
  pretty deploy --dry-run

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty lan`

```text
Usage:
  pretty lan <enable|disable|status>

manage same-network access

Enable, disable, or inspect explicit HTTP access from other devices on the same Wi-Fi or Ethernet network. Protected routes still require the daemon token.

Examples:
  pretty lan enable
  pretty lan status
  pretty lan disable

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty remote`

```text
Usage:
  pretty remote <enable|disable|status>

manage tailnet-only remote access

Enable, disable, or inspect the Tailscale Serve HTTPS endpoint used for Pretty remote access.

Examples:
  pretty remote enable
  pretty remote status
  pretty remote disable

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty token`

```text
Usage:
  pretty token

print the daemon authentication token

Read and print the local daemon token for use by an authorized Pretty client.

Examples:
  pretty token

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty backup`

```text
Usage:
  pretty backup <enable|now|status|decrypt> [options]

configure and run session backups

Enable scheduled backup storage, push a backup immediately, show backup status, or decrypt an encrypted backup. Enable requires --project and accepts --interval and --encrypt.

Examples:
  pretty backup enable --project my-project --interval 15m --encrypt
  pretty backup now
  pretty backup decrypt transcript.jsonl.enc
  pretty --json backup status

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty doctor`

```text
Usage:
  pretty doctor

diagnose daemon and session health

Report per-session health, spawn path, QoS state, and sessions which should be recreated.

Examples:
  pretty doctor
  pretty --json doctor

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty help`

```text
Usage:
  pretty help [command]

show top-level or command help

Show complete command help, or detailed help for one command. pretty <command> --help is equivalent.

Examples:
  pretty help
  pretty help run
  pretty recover --help

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```

## `pretty version`

```text
Usage:
  pretty version

print the CLI version

Print the Pretty CLI version and exit.

Examples:
  pretty version
  pretty --version

Global flags --json, --host, and --port must appear before the command.
```
